fact: August 22 sits exactly 234 days into the year - that's two-thirds of the way through!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when many spiders start spinning their biggest, most impressive webs to catch flying insects before winter.
The sun rises about 2 minutes later than it did just a week ago - summer is sneaking away!
This date falls smack in the middle of Virgo season, when people born under this sign are supposedly super organized and picky about everything.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, August 22 is deep winter - penguins are huddling together for warmth while we're still swimming!
Many fruit trees in temperate regions are getting so heavy with apples and pears that their branches start drooping toward the ground.
The moon can appear extra orange or red when it rises around this time of year because of all the dust and particles floating in late summer air.
Crickets chirp faster on August 22 than they will in just a few weeks - you can actually count their chirps to guess the temperature!
This date is roughly 30 days before the autumn equinox, when day and night become perfectly equal lengths.
In many places, morning dew starts getting heavier around now because the air holds more moisture as temperatures begin their slow drop.
Monarch butterflies in North America are starting to feel restless - their great-great-grandchildren will fly thousands of miles south in just a few weeks!
The Big Dipper constellation appears lower in the evening sky around August 22 than it did in June, slowly rotating as Earth orbits the sun.
